intent: Write a gamer-focused buying guide for 'upgrade from console to PC gaming' on a budget. You MUST research BOTH sources in depth: (1) the One Stop Market shopping site — browse video-games, electronics, and cell-phones-accessories categories. Collect data on at least 5 gaming-relevant products such as gaming headphones, controllers, mice, keyboards, monitor accessories, with price/rating/review_count from product detail pages. (2) Reddit — browse /f/gaming AND /f/technology for posts discussing gaming hardware, PC builds, peripheral recommendations, or gaming industry news; collect at least 4 posts with title/score/comment_count. The final report MUST: (a) categorize the available shopping products into 'essential' vs 'nice-to-have' tiers based on price and function, (b) integrate community voices — what real gamers on Reddit recommend or warn against, (c) identify any mismatches between shopping ratings and community reputation (e.g. heavily advertised products that the community dislikes), (d) deliver a concrete priority-ordered shopping list for a console-to-PC gamer with budget $400, citing every data point.
author_notes: Multi-category shopping + multi-forum reddit research. Requires synthesis of quantitative product data with qualitative community wisdom. Essential vs nice-to-have categorization forces prioritization reasoning.
